notes: This keeps the current high-score home gates and central braided ambush arena, then adds mirrored return tolls on C4/C6/G4/G6. The hypothesis is that the extra reversible fire tax near the outer return choices preserves full length credit while making route timing, healing, and guard decisions more valuable for higher-search MCTS than for greedy or low-search play.
conclusion: This map set a new high score of 68.66, beating braided-home-gates-v1 by preserving full horizon, side-fairness, turn-cap, and length credit while improving ladder separation from 0.630 to 0.687. The added C4/C6/G4/G6 fire tolls raised median length to 13 and increased weighted Elo gap to 232, driven by a much stronger mid-to-high gap of 326 Elo. The hunch was mostly right: the outer return tolls made higher-search timing and routing more valuable without creating replay risk, with only a 0.7% turn-cap rate and a fair 0.425 blue mirror winrate. The surprising downside is that mcts-low collapsed below greedy by 270 Elo, so the score is now carried almost entirely by the low-to-mid and especially mid-to-high search gains. A concrete next experiment would keep these outer return tolls but look for a small low-search stabilizer, such as softening one central fire tile, while trying not to reduce median length below 12.
